task_completion.visual_evidence: NextGen Connect Administrator UI or REST API payload showing a channel named exactly 'MRN_Quality_Firewall'.

task_completion.description: Source is configured to listen on TCP port 6661
task_completion.visual_evidence: Source tab showing a TCP Listener (or similar HL7 listener) configured with port 6661.

task_completion.description: Filter or routing logic checks for 6-digit numerical Patient ID
task_completion.visual_evidence: Filter tab, destination filters, or transformer scripts showing regex like '^\d{6}$' applied to PID-3.1, or equivalent logic checking for exactly 6 digits.

task_completion.description: Valid destination routes to /home/ga/valid with 'MRN-' prefix
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Destination configured as a File Writer to directory '/home/ga/valid'. The output template or mapping must show the Patient ID being prefixed with 'MRN-'.

task_completion.description: Quarantine destination routes to /home/ga/quarantine with error string
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Destination configured as a File Writer to directory '/home/ga/quarantine'. The output template must contain 'Invalid MRN detected: ' followed by the original ID.

task_completion.description: The channel is deployed and running
task_completion.visual_evidence: NextGen Connect Dashboard or Channels view showing 'MRN_Quality_Firewall' with a 'Deployed' or 'Started' status.

integrity.description: Agent used the actual NextGen Connect Administrator or REST API
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the NextGen Connect Administrator Java UI or a REST API client (like curl/Postman). The agent should not just be typing mock XML/configuration files in a basic text editor.

integrity.description: Agent did not manually create the output files to bypass the channel logic
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal history (if visible) should not show commands like 'echo' or 'touch' manually creating the expected output files in /home/ga/valid or /home/ga/quarantine.

integrity.description: Agent did not use the monitoring-only dashboard at localhost:8443 to configure the channel
integrity.visual_evidence: Configuration steps should be performed in the NextGen Connect Administrator client or via API, not attempted through the read-only web dashboard.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The channel must be named 'MRN_Quality_Firewall'. The source must be a TCP Listener on port 6661. The filter/routing logic should validate Patient IDs (PID-3.1) against the regex pattern ^\d{6}$ (exactly 6 digits). Valid messages go to '/home/ga/valid' with the ID prefixed by 'MRN-'. Invalid messages go to '/home/ga/quarantine' with the exact text 'Invalid MRN detected: <Original_ID>'. The channel must be deployed in NextGen Connect.
